mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and increases the relative attractiveness of short-duration fixed income, producing a persistent negative drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as rates remain high, independent of any specific trigger.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 in absolute value over any rolling 3-year window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader earnings downgrades and negative equity drift over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following triggered UNRATE increases are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between a high nominal federal funds rate and decelerating CPI inflation produces a rising real policy rate, which tightens financial conditions faster than nominal rates alone suggest, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month; neither series alone captures this effect because real rate level depends on their joint path.
candidate_trigger: implied real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) rises above prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Rank IC of joint real-rate proxy with forward 20d returns is not materially higher (by more than 0.02) than the best single-series IC, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline sharply and persistently only when the federal funds rate is simultaneously elevated above a restrictive threshold, indicating credit-sensitive demand destruction; under this regime, weak housing starts are followed by negative equity returns over the next quarter, whereas the same housing weakness in a low-rate regime carries no predictive power.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and HOUST declines >10% YoY, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the regime-conditional effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding real economic output and corporate throughput, which supports near-term earnings momentum and produces a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week, acting as a continuous always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly growth and forward 5d returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth below a threshold of +50,000 jobs per month signals emerging labor market weakness that precedes downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, only activating once the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following a PAYEMS sub-50k print are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or effect disappears after excluding recession-labeled periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate jointly indicate robust consumer demand supported by healthy labor income, reinforcing risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month; retail sales strength alone during a rising unemployment backdrop does not carry the same signal because it may reflect credit-financed spending near a downturn.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.4% while UNRATE flat or declining over 3 months
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with stable labor market
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-UNRATE signal shows no improvement in Rank IC over RSAFS alone (delta < 0.02), or forward 20d returns under triggered condition are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle defined by federal funds rate above 4.5%, further increases or persistence at elevated levels compress equity valuations more severely than in accommodative regimes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over one quarter; the same FEDFUNDS level in an easing or low-rate regime has no comparable negative effect, making the relationship regime-conditional.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rate
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns while FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly different from returns when FEDFUNDS <= 4.5% (p > 0.05), or Rank IC conditional on the regime is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
